differences.title: 1. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Pre-rendering via static generation or server-side rendering.
differences.rightDesc: Data loading happens at the route level on the server.

differences.title: 2. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Pages directory defines routes. API routes for endpoints.
differences.rightDesc: Nested routes and dynamic segments for complex UIs.

differences.title: 3. DATA MUTATION
differences.leftDesc: Dedicated API endpoints or newer Server Actions.
differences.rightDesc: Built-in handling for form submissions and mutations.

differences.title: 4. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Offers various rendering options for optimization.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily server-side rendering for faster initial loads.

differences.title: 5. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Leverages React context or external libraries.
differences.rightDesc: Uses browser APIs like Fetch, FormData, and request APIs.

differences.title: 6. DEPLOYMENT TARGET
differences.leftDesc: Highly integrated with Vercel for optimal performance.
differences.rightDesc: Deployable to any Node.js server or edge network.

differences.title: 7. ERROR HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Define custom pages for 404 and general errors.
differences.rightDesc: Handles errors within route modules for granular control.
